headline: Meta Acquires AI Agent Directory Moltbook, Signaling Platform Race Intensifies
summary: Meta has acquired Moltbook, an AI agent social network notable for its 'always-on-directory' connecting autonomous agents — despite the platform's viral growth being partly attributed to fabricated content. The acquisition signals Big Tech's move to control the discovery and identity layer for AI agents, a critical infrastructure chokepoint. For platform providers, whoever owns agent directories owns the trust graph — raising immediate questions about identity verification, provenance, and governance standards.
oci_implication: OCI's developer platform strategy must accelerate its own agent identity and registry capabilities — or risk ceding the agent orchestration layer to Meta's ecosystem. The Moltbook acquisition also highlights supply-chain and trust risks for enterprise customers running multi-agent workloads, reinforcing the value of OCI's sovereign and isolated deployment options for regulated industries requiring auditable agent provenance.
